_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage follows the curriculum in Runestone Foundations of Python Programming, Think Python, and OpenStax Introduction to Python Programming. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the sources guide topic selection only.
title: Testing
summary: Check that code gives the right result for normal and unusual inputs.
refresher: A test calls a small piece of code with known input and compares the result with the expected value. `assert` is useful for quick checks. `unittest` organizes larger test groups and reports which case failed.

blocks.body: Running a program once shows only that one path worked. Tests save examples of correct behavior so they can be repeated after the code changes.

blocks.caption: Each assertion states one expectation. If an expression is false, Python raises AssertionError and points to the failed check.
blocks.code:
def is_even(number):
    return number % 2 == 0

assert is_even(4) is True
assert is_even(7) is False
print("checks passed")

blocks.body: Good tests include an ordinary case, a boundary, and an unusual but valid case. For a list function, that may mean several items, one item, and an empty list.

blocks.caption: A test should check a result, not repeat the function's calculation in a more complicated form. Direct expected values are easier to review.
blocks.wrong: assert total(nums) == sum(x for x in nums)

blocks.title: A passing test can still be weak
blocks.body: A test proves only the case it checks. If every test uses positive numbers, a bug involving zero or negative values can remain hidden.

blocks.prompt: Which input is the best boundary case for a function that finds the largest list item?
choices: A list with ten random items
A list already used in the demo
blocks.why: One item is the smallest valid list for this function. It checks whether the code wrongly assumes that a second item exists.

blocks.body: The unittest module groups checks into methods. `setUp` can prepare fresh data before each test, and methods beginning with `test_` are discovered by the test runner.

blocks.prompt: What happens when an assert expression is false?
choices: Python returns False from the program
blocks.why: An assert statement stops at a false condition and raises AssertionError. Test tools catch that error and show which expectation failed.

blocks.title: Testing checklist
items: Test one clear behavior at a time.
Use fixed inputs with obvious expected results.
Include normal, boundary, and unusual cases.
Give tests names that describe the behavior.
Run the tests again after every meaningful change.
